description_long: This is a classic, ornate teapot with a bulbous body, a tall, rounded lid topped with a knob, and a gracefully curved spout. The handle is large and rounded, providing a comfortable grip, and is attached at the upper and lower portions of the body. The surface is decorated with a sepia-toned architectural motif, depicting domed rooftops and spires, likely inspired by European cityscapes. The teapot appears to be made of glazed ceramic, giving it a slightly glossy, hard finish. The decorative pattern wraps around the body, making it visually striking and suitable for display as well as functional use.
description: An ornate ceramic teapot with a domed lid, curved spout, and sepia architectural motifs wrapping around its glossy surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spout on the left, handle on the right, and a detailed architectural motif with domes and rooftops.
description_view_1: Rear view with the handle centered, less visible decoration, and the lid's knob prominent at the top.
description_view_2: Side view with the spout on the left, handle on the right, and a detailed facade of a building with towers and crosses.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the handle centered, minimal visible decoration, and the lid's knob at the top.
attribute_spout_orientation: Curved, angled slightly upward, positioned at the front left of the body.
attribute_handle_design: Large, rounded loop handle attached at the upper and lower body, thick and ergonomic.
attribute_lid_presence: Present; domed with a rounded knob on top, fits snugly.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Sepia-toned architectural motifs depicting domes and spires, wrapping around the body with high contrast against the white background.
